Name: Pikachu
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: If the leaves on its head are pruned with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Pidgey
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Mega Pidgeot ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If all of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can’t be a fixed destination.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Ditto
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon's attacks, except any Pokémon ex, and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It can freely recombine its own cellular structure to transform into other life-forms.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can’t help but grovel before it.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your Active [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Onix
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

Name: Genesect
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

Name: Mega Latios ex
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Mudkip
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When it uses its large tail fin, it picks up speed rapidly in the water. It is strong in spite of its small size.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out the electricity stored inside its body to create noises.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Mega Medicham ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage. This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

Name: Cinderace
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Using a ball that it made out of flames, Cinderace blinds its opponents and keeps them at bay.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Flavor-Text: In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

Name: Quaxly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon converts nutrients into oil, which it stores in the fruit on its head. It can easily go a whole week without eating or drinking.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Tatsugiri
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

Name: Frigibax
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

Name: Tinkaton
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Gigaton Hammer.
Flavor-Text: The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Paldean Clodsire
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.

Name: Chien-Pao ex
Effects: Discard all [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 130 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Electrike
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Flavor-Text: It overwhelms enemies with its prized tusks, which are sharp enough to cut through a metal transmission tower in one strike.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.

Name: Wigglytuff
Flavor-Text: The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can’t stop touching it.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of 1 of your [R] Pokémon, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can't use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Meloetta
Ability-Effect: At the beginning of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random [P] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Zygarde
Flavor-Text: Born when around 10% of Zygarde’s cells have been gathered from all over, this form is skilled in close-range combat.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

Name: Zygarde ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Zygarde ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Heracross ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Castform changes to this form when it basks in bright sunlight. When you touch its glowing skin, it feels all dried out!

Name: Tepig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a ravenous glutton. It uses its excellent sense of smell to find food, then cooks it to a crisp before eating.

Name: Dwebble
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −10 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

Name: Zarude
Effects: If any of your [D] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

Name: Treecko
Flavor-Text: Treecko can quickly scale even vertical surfaces. It senses humidity with its tail to predict the next day’s weather.

Name: Gallade
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

Name: Meowscarada ex
Effects: Choose a spot from among your opponent's Active Spot and Bench. At the end of your opponent's next turn, do 70 damage to the Pokémon in the spot you chose.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can’t be seen when it melts away into water.

Name: Ceruledge ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Any chips in its blades would prove fatal for it. After each battle, it diligently maintains its blades using its favorite sharpening stone.

Name: Dudunsparce
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Dunsparce during this turn, discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

Name: Floragato
Flavor-Text: The hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Take a random Energy from among [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], and [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Materials from gems it has eaten float to the surface of its body and can form an infinite number of patterns among individuals.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

Name: Froslass
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Raichu
Flavor-Text: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on riverbed boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

Name: Hisuian Zorua
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Emolga
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.
